findings.description: SECONDARY and TERTIARY palette tiers (Sky Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Coral, Light Blue, Golden Sand, Graphite, White) are entirely uncovered by any candidate rule; only the PRIMARY tier is represented, leaving 8 of 11 documented colors and two full tiers unclustered.

payload.note: Add separate rules (or split existing rule) for SECONDARY tier (Sky Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy) and TERTIARY tier (Coral, Light Blue, Golden Sand, Graphite, White) with tier-specific scope.

findings.description: Source units are purely descriptive (a palette table with hex/RGB/CMYK/Pantone values) with no directive or normative language such as 'must use' or 'only'. Tagging the rule as hard_constraint/must overstates the enforcement strength implied by the source; should likely be a lower hardness (e.g., guideline/soft_constraint) with polarity 'should' unless directive language exists elsewhere.

Rule lacks governance facets (e.g., approval authority, exception process, scope of application such as digital vs print) that would normally accompany a hard_constraint color rule; only color values are captured without any compliance/enforcement metadata.

The heading '### Complete Palette' (u_0000) that scopes the entire section (all three tiers) is not referenced by any rule, losing the overall grouping context that ties PRIMARY/SECONDARY/TERTIARY together as one complete palette definition.
